message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Purpose</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To evaluate the outcome of patients with COVID-19 triggered CRS treated with Therapeutic Plasma Exchange (TPE) as compared to propensity score matched (PSM)-controls not receiving TPE.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Material and methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Using PS 1:1 matching, 90 patients were assigned 2 groups (45 receiving TPE and 45 controls). Forced matching and covariate matching was done to overcome bias between two groups.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Median age was 60 years (range 32-73 in TPE and 37-75 in non-TPE group), p= 0.325. Median duration of symptoms 7 days (range 3-22 days in TPE and 3-20 days in non-TPE), p=0.266. Disease severity in both groups was 6.6% moderate, 44.4% severe and 49% critical. Twenty-eight-day survival was significantly superior in TPE group (91.1%) as compared to PS-matched controls (61.5%), HR 0.21, 95% CI for HR 0.09-0.53, log rank 0.002. Median duration of hospitalization was significantly reduced in TPE treated group as compared to non-TPE controls 10 days and 15 days respectively (p< 0.01). CRS resolution time was also significantly reduced in TPE treated group (6 days vs. 12 days) (p< 0.001).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Use of TPE is associated with superior overall survival, early resolution of CRS and time to discharge as compared to standard therapy for COVID-19 triggered CRS.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="simple">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>□</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) plays a pivotal role in pathophysiology and progression to severe and critical disease in patients with COVID-19.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>□</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>Beyond supportive care, there are currently no proven effective treatment options (including Tocilizumab) for coronavirus disease (COVID-19)</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>□</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>Therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) may dampen CRS of COVID-19 by removing circulating cytokines and toxins</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>□</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>TPE is the cheapest of all novel treatments available so far to treat severe to critical COVID-19</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>□</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>By intervening early with TPE, mortality and morbidity associated with COVID-19 triggered CRS can be reduced</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: “PLEXIT - Therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) for Covid-19 cytokine release storm (CRS), a retrospective propensity matched control study”
